title: Bridge Essay: Impaired motor-to-sensory transformation mediates auditory hallucinations.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nested Agency** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Nested agency — voluntary action, free will as constructed experience, intentional binding.

**Impaired motor-to-sensory transformation mediates auditory hallucinations.**. PLoS Biol.. PMID: 39361912. DOI: 10.1371/journal.pbio.3002836. Distinguishing reality from hallucinations requires efficient monitoring of agency. It has been hypothesized that acopy of motor signals, termed efference copy (EC) or corollary discharge (CD), suppresses sensory responses to yield asense of agency; impairment of the inhibitory function leads to hallucinations.
